---
title: "Teaching"
---

## Teaching Philosophy

I view literature and cultural studies as opportunities for students to examine how narratives shape ideas of identity, history, and social experience. My teaching emphasizes close reading, critical discussion, and the relationship between literary texts, visual media, and contemporary cultural questions.

## Courses Taught

### Instructor of Record

**Seoul Women's University**

- *Contested Americas: Culture, Media, and Society*  
  Fall 2026–

- *Korean Modern Literature and Film*  
  Fall 2026–

**Binghamton University (SUNY)**

- WRIT 111: *Inquiry and Academic Writing*  
  Fall 2024–Spring 2026

- Writing 110  
  Summer 2025

- *Hip-Hop: Culture of Voice*  
  Winter 2025; Summer 2024; Summer 2023

- *Best Picture: Oscar Winners*  
  Winter 2023; Winter 2022

- *British Literature and Film*  
  Fall 2021; Fall 2020

## Teaching Assistant Experience

**Binghamton University (SUNY)**

- *DIDA: Interactive Fiction* | Educational Opportunity Program  
  Summer 2024

- *British Literature I*  
  Spring 2020

- *British Literature II*  
  Fall 2019

- *Children's Literature*  
  Spring 2019; Spring 2018

- *Contesting American Identities*  
  Fall 2018

- *Native American Folklore & Literature*  
  Fall 2017
